name: Code Quest
description: Drag-and-drop maze game that teaches programming fundamentals — sequencing, loops, conditionals. Dot guides students through escalating hints and tracks mastery per concept.
useCase: Introduce K-8 students to programming concepts (sequencing, loops, conditionals) through a block-based game. Dot can grade attempts, surface hints, and track mastery via tool calls.
author: Jason Baddley

tools.name: evaluateAttempt
tools.description: Runs the student's current block program against the active maze level and returns whether it solved the maze, plus a failure signal if not.

tools.name: generateHint
tools.description: Returns a tiered hint for the current level. Level 1 is Socratic, 2 is directive, 3 is a guided walkthrough.

tools.name: getMastery
tools.description: Returns the student's current mastery scores (0..1) across sequencing, loops, conditionals, variables.

tools.name: setLevel
tools.description: Switches the active level. Only call when the student explicitly asks to advance. Progression order: robots-first-steps, long-march, zig-zag, staircase, locked-door. The locked-door level has a key and a locked door (the door only opens once the key is collected).

tools.name: getLevelLesson
tools.description: Returns the lesson content for the current level: the concept being taught (e.g. 'Loops'), a kid-friendly explanation, three Socratic starter questions to open with, and an 'aha moment' to celebrate with the student after they solve it. Call this whenever a level starts or when you want to introduce or revisit the concept.

tools.name: buildLevel
tools.description: Design and inject a brand-new custom maze level. YOU (Dot) are the level designer — use your creativity to craft a grid, choose walls, place start/goal, pick concepts, and write hints + lesson content. Call this when the student asks to 'build a level', 'make a harder challenge', 'create a maze for me', etc. The PowerUp validates the maze and switches to it automatically. If validation fails, the error message will tell you what to fix — adjust and retry.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier, e.g. 'the-gauntlet' or 'triple-loop'. Must not conflict with existing level IDs.

name.description: Short display name shown in the header, e.g. 'The Gauntlet'.

goal.description: One sentence describing the challenge, e.g. 'Navigate three corridors using only move-until-wall.'

grid.description: Row-major 2D array of cells. Each cell is 'start', 'empty', 'wall', 'goal', or 'key'. MUST have exactly one 'start' and one 'goal'. Goal must be reachable from start.

startDir.description: Which direction the robot faces at the start cell.

concepts.description: Concepts this level targets. Use 'sequencing', 'loops', 'conditionals', 'variables', 'repeatUntil', 'nestedLoops'. These control which blocks appear in the toolbox.

maxSteps.description: Max instructions before the run is aborted (protects against infinite loops). Use 400 for normal levels, 600 for large grids.

hints.description: Three progressive hints. '1' is a Socratic question, '2' is a directive tip, '3' is a step-by-step walkthrough.

lesson.description: Lesson content Dot uses to introduce the level.

explanation.description: 1-2 sentence plain-English explanation for a 3rd-7th grader.

starterQuestions.description: Three Socratic questions to open with before the student starts coding.

ahaMoment.description: The one-liner the student should understand after solving.

tools.name: listModules
tools.description: Returns all curriculum modules with their metadata (title, description, concepts, grade band, estimated time, prerequisites). Does not include level data. Use this to tell a student what learning paths are available or to recommend a next module.

includeUnpublished.description: If true, include draft/unpublished modules. Default: false.

tools.name: getModule
tools.description: Returns full metadata for a specific curriculum module, including its level list (IDs, names, concepts) and narrative arc. Does not include full level data.

tools.name: setModule
tools.description: Activates a curriculum module, switching the student to its first level (or a specific level). Use this to move a student between learning paths or start a new module.

levelId.description: Optional: jump to a specific level within the module. Defaults to the first level.

tools.name: createModule
tools.description: Creates a new curriculum module with the given metadata. The module starts with no levels; use addLevelToModule (or buildLevel) to populate it. Returns the new module ID.

objectives.description: "Students will be able to…" learning objectives.

prerequisites.description: Module IDs that should be completed before this one.

tools.name: addLevelToModule
tools.description: Adds an existing level (built-in or custom) to a curriculum module by level ID. Use after buildLevel to incorporate a newly created level into a module.

moduleId.description: The module to add the level to.

levelId.description: ID of the level to add.

position.description: 0-based index to insert at. Omit to append.

tools.name: exportModule
tools.description: Returns the complete JSON definition of a module as a string — including all embedded levels, metadata, standards, assessments, and narrative. Ready to save as a .json file.
